title: Denied Wing Backdoor
game_context: 4-on-4 half-court shell, ball reversal from the left slot, 14 on the shot clock.
possession_setup: The ball is at the left slot. You are the right wing. The defense is denying your reversal — your defender's hand and foot are in the passing lane.
decision_moment: Your defender steps into the passing lane, hand and foot extended, chest between ball and receiver.
visible_cue: x2 has hand and foot in the lane, hips opened toward the sideline, chest between ball and receiver.
prompt: Your defender is blocking the pass. What do you do?

choices.label: Cut behind him to the basket.
choices.feedback_text: Best read. The defender is blocking the pass, so the basket is open. Cut behind him.

choices.label: Step back out and ask for the ball.
choices.feedback_text: Okay. You kept the play alive, but you missed the open layup behind your defender.
choices.partial_feedback_text: Stepping back keeps the play alive. The better read was to cut behind him for the layup.

choices.label: Stay still and call for the ball.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. Your defender is in the way. If you stand still, he steals the pass.

choices.label: Cut in front of the defender.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. Cutting in front lets him follow you. Cut behind him instead.

best_read: Plant the outside foot and back-cut behind x2 to the front of the rim; passer leads you to the layup before x4 can rotate.
acceptable_reads: V-cut out to a deeper catch point. Possession kept, but the layup window is missed.
bad_reads: Stay on the wing and call for the ball — defender deflects the reversal.
Slow front cut — defender rides the route and the window closes.
common_miss_reason: Loyalty to the spot. Wings keep fighting for the assigned catch even after the defender has visibly given up the basket to take away the pass.
why_best_read_works: The defender is guarding the pass, not the basket. The denial removes the wing catch but opens the layup window behind it — the back-cut punishes the very stance that denied the reversal.
decoder_teaching_point: When your defender blocks the pass, the space behind him is open. Cut there.

feedback.correct: Good read. Your defender blocked the pass, so the space behind him was open. You took the layup.
feedback.partial: Stepping back kept the play alive. Next time, watch his body. If he is blocking the pass, cut behind him for the layup.
feedback.wrong: Watch his body. If your defender is blocking the pass, the space behind him is open. Cut behind him.

self_review_checklist: Did I see his hand and foot blocking the pass?
Did I cut behind him, not in front?
Did I cut hard, like I wanted the layup?
Did I show my hands at the rim?

coach_validation.notes: Backdoor against denial is universally taught. Later confirm whether the default youth pass should be bounce, lead, or chest-fake-to-bounce.

source_research_basis: Universally taught youth-level read; backdoor against denial is system-independent.
explanation_md: **The Backdoor Window.** Your defender has his hand and foot in the way of the pass. That means he is blocking the pass, not the basket. The space behind him is open. Take a hard step toward the ball, then cut behind him to the rim. Show your hands so your teammate can pass it. Watch his body, not your spot.

wrongDemos.caption: You kept the ball. You missed the open layup.
The defender followed your cut. No layup.
